dependency_notes: S3 -> S4 is a hard code dependency: auto-accept correlates on the authenticated sender, which only exists once S3's authenticated unwrap lands (architecture.md 'Seams'). S3 itself is two changes (sign the join-request seal in joinRequestSender.ts; enforce seal verification in welcomeSubscription.ts) that MUST ship in the same story -- enforcing verification alone would drop every join request, so they are not split. S1 -> S2 is a product-level ordering only (the gate guarantees a name exists before send), not a code dependency -- the handler must tolerate a missing requesterName regardless, so S2 is unit-testable in isolation without S1. S2 and S3 both edit joinRequestSender.ts but in disjoint functions (buildJoinRequestRumor vs buildGiftWrap); no logical dependency, only a same-file edit to be aware of during sequential implementation. S5 depends on all four prior stories because its e2e case exercises the full nameless-invitee path end to end, and its AC-8 amendment documents the outcome of S2/S3's design (kind-0 is never the source of the nickname).
mock_resolution_plan: No temporary mocks introduced. Stories are implemented sequentially (per commands/feature.md non-goals), so S4 consumes S3's real, already-landed authenticated-unwrap output directly rather than mocking it.

seams.data: Authenticated sender identity produced by unwrapGiftWrap after S3's enforcement lands

invariants: authenticated is true only when the seal's schnorr signature verifies AND rumor.pubkey === seal.pubkey
pubkey is always the seal pubkey; it is never the raw, self-claimed rumor.pubkey
a message failing either check yields authenticated: false and MUST NOT satisfy any trust decision, including AC-AUTO-4's correlation match

stories.name: name-gate
stories.title: Name gate on the join card
stories.description: Adds a blocking name gate to JoinRequestCard for nameless users: renders a name input and a disabled Request-to-Join button until hasShareableName(profile.nickname) is satisfied, reactively. A user who already has a name sees today's card unchanged. The submitted name is persisted only through the existing saveProfile chokepoint. New en/de i18n keys cover the field label, helper text, and disabled-button state.

includes: JoinRequestCard.tsx: evaluate the name gate only after the existing hydration/identity, invalid-admin-npub, and already-member guards (lines 43-70)
Render a name <Input> and keep 'Request to Join' disabled while !hasShareableName(profile.nickname); clicking the disabled button MUST NOT call sendJoinRequest
Keep the group name visible on the card while the name input is shown
Gate is reactive: entering/clearing a name toggles the button live, not only on mount
Submit persists the name via saveProfile (ProfileContext) exclusively; the name is then readable on /profile
New i18n keys (en+de) for the name field label, helper text, and disabled-button state, following the copy.groups.joinRequest* grouping
excludes: No change to the existing hydration/identity/invalid-admin-npub/already-member gate logic itself, only an additional gate evaluated after them
No redirect to /profile (DD-2) -- the field renders in place on the join card
No nickname write path other than saveProfile

stories.notes: lighter_path is false: file targets (JoinRequestCard.tsx, i18n.ts) are both .tsx/.ts, i.e. files_code is non-empty, which alone forces lighter_path = false regardless of AC completeness.

stories.name: nickname-transport
stories.title: Requester nickname transport to the admin
stories.description: Carries the requester's nickname inside the existing gift-wrapped join-request rumor under a new requesterName field (distinct from the group's own name field), parses it tolerantly on receive, caps it at 32 UTF-8 bytes independently of the send-side cap, and confirms the admin's pending row co-renders the nickname alongside the truncated npub (never replacing it). No kind-0 lookup is introduced anywhere in this path.

includes: buildJoinRequestRumor (joinRequestSender.ts): add requesterName to the rumor content, distinct from the existing group-name-carrying name field
parseJoinRequestContent (joinRequestHandler.ts): treat an absent/empty/non-string requesterName as undefined without rejecting the rumor
handleJoinRequest: populate PendingJoinRequest.nickname from the parsed value, capped at 32 UTF-8 bytes on receive -- the security control, independent of any send-side cap
Nickname renders as literal text (no markup/HTML execution) in the admin's row

Verify PendingRequestRow already co-renders nickname and truncated npub (exploration.json: this appears structurally present at lines 30-41) before editing -- do not rewrite the row on the assumption the npub is missing
No code path in this story resolves or falls back to a kind-0 lookup for the nickname; the nickname reaches the admin exclusively inside the existing NIP-59 gift wrap addressed to the admin's pubkey
excludes: No new event kind, no new transport, no new relay publish
No seal-signing change (kind-13 seal signing is S3's concern, in the same file's buildGiftWrap function)
No change to admin approve/deny behavior

stories.notes: AC-SEC-3 (authenticated npub in the same row) is owned by S3, not here, because its guarantee and test depend on the authenticated-unwrap enforcement, not on this story's parsing/rendering work.

stories.name: authenticated-unwrap
stories.title: Authenticated gift-wrap unwrap and signed join-request seal
stories.description: Two changes that ship together as one unit: (a) signs the join-request kind-13 seal in joinRequestSender.ts's buildGiftWrap using the audited nostr-tools/nip59 helpers already used by the DM path, and (b) enforces seal-signature verification plus rumor.pubkey === seal.pubkey in the shared unwrapGiftWrap for both kind-444 (Welcome) and kind-21059 (join request). Corrects the stale doc comment claiming the rumor itself is signed. A failed check never throws the subscription; it degrades to the existing uncorrelated/dropped handling with authenticated-sender status false.

includes: joinRequestSender.ts buildGiftWrap: sign the kind-13 seal with the sender's real key (prefer nostr-tools/nip59 seal/wrap helpers over extending the hand-rolled construction) before encrypting it into the wrap

welcomeSubscription.ts unwrapGiftWrap: verify the seal's schnorr signature and assert rumor.pubkey === seal.pubkey, for BOTH kind-444 and kind-21059, matching unwrapAndOpen's semantics (directMessages.ts:230-274)

Failure handling: a message failing either check does not throw/halt the subscription -- a Welcome degrades to the uncorrelated path (pending queue, AC-AUTO-3), a join request is dropped and produces no PendingJoinRequest
The inviterPubkeyHex recorded for a queued Welcome is the authenticated seal pubkey, never the raw rumor.pubkey (closes the pre-existing forged-Welcome display bug)
Correct the stale doc comment (~welcomeSubscription.ts lines 199-211) asserting the rumor is signed -- NIP-59 rumors are unsigned; the seal authenticates
Both halves (send-side signing, receive-side enforcement) land in this single story per architecture.md's seam rule -- enforcing (b) without (a) would drop every join request
excludes: No correlation or auto-accept logic (S4's concern) -- this story only makes the authenticated sender identity trustworthy
No change to PendingInvitations rendering (AC-INVITE-4 untouched)
No change to the requesterName field itself (S2's concern)

stories.notes: Security-critical story: two coupled changes across joinRequestSender.ts and welcomeSubscription.ts must ship atomically, per architecture.md's explicit 'MUST NOT split' rule. Also touches joinRequestSender.ts's buildGiftWrap, a different function from S2's buildJoinRequestRumor edit in the same file -- a merge-order note for the implementer, not a logical dependency.

stories.name: auto-accept
stories.title: Outbound record and authenticated-admin auto-accept
stories.description: Introduces a new bounded, expiring store of outbound join-request records ({nonce, adminPubkeyHex, groupName, sentAt}) and, in welcomeSubscription.ts, correlates an authenticated Welcome sender against that store to auto-accept without user interaction -- disambiguating by pre-join group data when more than one record shares an admin. Extracts a shared join core so auto-accept and manual accept both route through joinGroupFromWelcome. Depends on S3: correlation requires the authenticated sender identity S3 produces.

includes: New outboundJoinRequests.ts: IndexedDB store of {nonce, adminPubkeyHex, groupName, sentAt}, keyed by nonce, mirroring joinRequestStorage.ts/inviteLinkStorage.ts conventions
Write a record only on successful sendJoinRequest; no record on a failed send
Bound the store at 256 total records; TTL >= 7 days; an expired record does not correlate
welcomeSubscription.ts: correlate on the AuthenticatedUnwrapResult's pubkey against unexpired records' adminPubkeyHex (never on nonce, which never reaches the Welcome)

Disambiguate multiple records sharing an admin by comparing the Welcome's pre-join group data (readWelcomeMarmotGroupData) against each candidate's stored groupName -- exactly one match auto-accepts and consumes that record; zero or multiple matches are treated as uncorrelated
Extract a shared join core (joinGroupFromWelcome + record cleanup) called by both manual accept and auto-accept; no enqueue-then-immediately-dequeue shortcut
Consume (remove) only the disambiguated matched record after a correlated auto-accept; other records for the same admin survive
An uncorrelated Welcome (no match, expired match, or failed authentication) is enqueued exactly as today, requiring explicit Accept
Verify approveJoinRequest/denyJoinRequest (MarmotContext.tsx) and the PendingInvitations component's rendered content are unchanged by this story
excludes: No change to the admin's approve/deny step
No nonce-based correlation on the Welcome side
No change to what PendingInvitations renders (still truncated pubkey, timestamp, Accept, Decline only)

stories.notes: Hard dependency on S3 (architecture.md 'S3 -> S4'). owning_module is 'Outbound records' (the new module this story creates) rather than 'Welcome/join unwrap', which S3 already owns -- architecture.md explicitly assigns both S3 and S4 to touch welcomeSubscription.ts sequentially, so this is documented shared module ownership, not a conflict.

stories.name: e2e-and-ac8-amendment
stories.title: Nameless-path e2e coverage and AC-8 amendment
stories.description: Adds the nameless-invitee e2e case to groups-invite-link.spec.ts (gate blocks, name entered, admin sees the name, admin approves, invitee lands in the group without a second click), asserts the existing named-user case and its no-name-input behavior still hold, and amends specs/epic-group-invite-links/acceptance-criteria.md AC-8 to remove the kind-0 clause this epic obsoletes, recording the change in that epic's Amendments section.

includes: New e2e case: nameless invitee -> gate blocks -> name entered -> admin's pending row shows the name -> admin approves -> invitee lands in the group with no second click, asserted via the group card appearing without any Accept interaction
Existing named-user path continues to pass unmodified, plus an explicit assertion that no name input renders for that case
All peer publishes in new e2e code go through the app's own helpers via a second browser.newContext() -- no raw WebSocket to strfry
The e2e gate for this epic is the full suite (make test-e2e-all), not a filtered subset

Amend specs/epic-group-invite-links/acceptance-criteria.md AC-8 to remove the kind-0-resolution clause and record the amendment in that epic's ## Amendments section, per this epic's spec.md #Relationship-to-Other-Epics
excludes: No production code changes beyond what S1-S4 already delivered

stories.notes: Bundles the AC-8 doc amendment with e2e test code in one story because the amendment has no AC ID of its own in this epic's acceptance-criteria.md, and every story needs >=1 AC from this epic's list (schema minItems) without duplicating an AC's ownership across stories. Splitting the amendment into its own story would need to borrow an AC-ETE-* or AC-NAME-5 id already exclusively owned elsewhere, which the epic's 'exactly one story per AC' rule forbids. Since the spec.ts file target is code (non-markdown extension), this story is not lighter_path-eligible as a whole even though the amendment sub-task in isolation would be pure markdown.
